rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes to avoid scope errors.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference opaque Boolean or enumerated data types in numeric comparisons.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE identifiers are defined within the visible scope of the annex where they are used.
Ensure AGREE assumptions and guarantees are placed in the correct component scope (type vs. implementation).
Remove AGREE clauses that reference invalid or unsupported data types, such as opaque Booleans in range comparisons.
When diagnostics point to missing references, trace and resolve the root cause rather than adding new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reside in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within valid AADL component scopes.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to prevent type errors.
Implementation-level assignments and bindings must be in the component implementation annex, not the type annex.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes; move them to the correct scope.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric forms in AGREE expressions.
Do not add new architecture to resolve AGREE diagnostics; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
